exercises.name: High Plank Alternating Arm & Leg Lift
exercises.description:
1.Support your body off the floor, resting on your toes and forearms.

2.Raise one arm and the opposite leg straight up off the floor. Try to maintain your body in a straight line and keep your back neutral/flat.

3.Hold briefly, then lower back your leg and arm to the floor returning to starting position. 

4.Repeat with the other arm and opposite leg. Alternate sides with each rep.

exercises.name: High Plank Alternating Single Arm Raise
exercises.description: In a high plank position, raise one arm forward at a time, alternating while maintaining stability through the core.

exercises.name: High Plank Alternating Single Leg Lift
exercises.description:
1.Support your body off the floor, resting on your toes and forearms.

2.Raise one leg straight up off the floor, keeping your body in a straight line and your back neutral/flat.

3.Lower back your leg to the floor returning to starting position.

4.Raise the opposite leg. Alternate sides with each rep.

exercises.name: High Plank Jacks
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, ensure that you keep your abdominals on and hips higher in a neutral position. That’ll prevent any lower back sway and back injuries. 

2) When you get into the plank position, set your hands underneath your shoulders. 

3) From there, alternate your feet jumping out and coming back together. 

4) Repeat.

exercises.name: High Plank Shoulder Taps
exercises.description: In a high plank position, tap your opposite shoulder with each hand while maintaining a stable core and avoiding rocking your hips.

exercises.name: High Plank Single Leg Lift Hold
exercises.description: Hold a high plank position and lift one leg off the ground, maintaining balance and keeping your core engaged.

exercises.name: High Plank T Rotation
exercises.description: Stand with feet hip-width apart. Raise arms to shoulder height and rotate your torso to each side, engaging the core and hips.

exercises.name: High Side Plank from Knees
exercises.description:
1. Start on your side, with your body body propped up on your palm, and your knees stacked with knees bent. 

2. Drive your hips forward, while keeping a rigid abdomen. 

3. Hold this position while breathing and staying tense.

4. Repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: High Side Plank from Knees With Leg Lift
exercises.description:
1. Start by lying on the mat on your side, with your body propped up on the hand and knee of the bottom leg. 

2. Drive your hips up off the mat, and push your hips forward until they are fully extended. 

3. Hold this position, with your top leg fully extended and abdominals tense. 

4. Continue to breathe and hold this position.

5. Repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: High Side Plank with Abduction
exercises.alternateName: Single Leg High Side Plank leg lift
exercises.description:
1.Lie down on your side. Place your feet together on top of each other. Prop yourself up, raising your hips and your arm fully extended.

2.Raise the other arm towards the ceiling, and your one leg up in the air, creating a 45-degree angle.

3. Hold briefly then return to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Hinge to T-Rotation
exercises.description: Hinge forward at the hips, then rotate your torso to one side, reaching one arm toward the ceiling. Repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Hip Airplane
exercises.description:
Stand on one leg with a slight bend in the knee and engage your core for balance. Use your arm opposite the standing leg to lightly hold onto a platform or bench for support if needed. 

Hinge forward at the hips, extending your non-supporting leg straight behind you while simultaneously rotating your torso and arms to one side, forming a "T" shape with your body. 

Maintain control and stability throughout the movement.

exercises.name: Hip taps
Hip taps 2
Hip Thrust Machine
exercises.alternateName: Loaded Glute bridge machine, Glute drive machine
exercises.description: Sit against a bench with a barbell over hips. Drive through heels to lift hips until body is in a straight line. Squeeze glutes at the top.

exercises.name: Hip Thrust to March
exercises.description:
Sit on the floor with your upper back resting on a bench or elevated surface, knees bent, and feet flat on the ground. Press through your heels to lift your hips into a bridge, forming a straight line from shoulders to knees. 

While holding the bridge, alternate lifting each knee toward your chest in a marching motion, keeping your hips elevated and stable throughout.

exercises.name: Hips Elevated Dumbbell Pullover
exercises.description:
Lie on your back with your upper back resting on a bench and feet flat on the floor, knees bent at 90 degrees. 

Hold a single dumbbell with both hands above your chest, arms fully extended, palms facing each other. Lift your hips into a bridge position so your body forms a straight line from shoulders to knees. 

Keeping your hips elevated and core tight, slowly lower the dumbbell back over your head in a controlled motion until you feel a stretch in your chest and lats. 

Pull the dumbbell back to the starting position above your chest while maintaining the bridge throughout the movement.

exercises.name: Hollow Body Hold
exercises.description:
1.Lie on the floor with your legs straight, feet, knees, and legs together, arms extended overhead.

2.Raise your legs and upper body up slightly to where only your buttocks are touching the floor.

3.Hold briefly then slowly lower your body back to the floor returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Hollow Body Hold Flutter Kicks
exercises.description:
1) For this exercise, you’ll come up in a V position. 

2) Sitting on your sit bones and your pelvis. 

3) Make sure that your abdominals are tight and pulled in, not leaning back, loading your lower back. 

4) From there, kick your legs in an alternate pattern.

exercises.name: Hollow Rock to V-Up
exercises.description:
Lie on your back with arms extended overhead and legs straight, forming a hollow body position with lower back pressed lightly into the floor. 

Rock forward and backward slightly to engage the core, then simultaneously lift your legs and torso into a V-up, reaching your hands toward your feet. 

Slowly return to the hollow position and repeat continuously.

exercises.name: Horizontal Cable Rotation
exercises.description: Stand perpendicular to the cable. With both hands, rotate your torso away from the machine, arms extended. Return to center with control.
